category: Footer decaf link mistaken for product decaf

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. Exact support includes: product title, roaster, roaster location/country, origin country/region, process, variety, producer/farm, altitude, roast level ('Filter roast'), harvest period, tasting notes, producer text, listed price/currency, 250g bag size/package grams, one-time purchase context, and availability ('In stock'). Enum inferences also hold: not a blend (single-origin named coffee with one origin/variety), not espresso (explicitly 'Filter roast' and filter brew guidance), not decaf (main product is not decaf; decaf only appears in unrelated recommendations/footer), and not coferment/infused (described as washed process with no infusion/co-fermentation mentioned).

realism.reasons: This reads very plausibly like a real specialty coffee roaster product page. The structure is highly typical: breadcrumb, product title, price, size selector, stock/shipping note, roast style, tasting notes, origin data, process/variety/altitude/harvest fields, producer story, brew suggestion, roaster location, related products, and footer links. The voice is consistent with specialty coffee copy—clean, restrained, and specific without sounding overblown. Origin details are coherent: Huila/Palestina, Pink Bourbon, washed process, 1,700–1,850 masl, and a late-2024 harvest all fit a contemporary Colombia offering. The tasting notes and description align well with Pink Bourbon and a filter roast. The production description is believable and not overtechnical. The variant data block also looks realistic: sensible IDs, SKUs, names, gram weights, and pricing with a normal 250g-to-1kg discount curve. Minor tiny tells only: 'Size: 250g' appears as fixed text while a 1kg variant exists, and 'default_for_inference' is not something a consumer-facing page would show, but since this is framed as embedded variant data in an html-to-text rendering, that is acceptable. Overall, it is very close to indistinguishable from a real roaster page.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Finca El Mirador, Luis Anibal Calderón
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting notes: bergamot, white peach, panela, jasmine

In the cup we find a floral, tea-like structure with peach sweetness and a clean panela finish.
label.coffee.producer_text: Luis Anibal Calderón and his family farm Finca El Mirador in Huila, where they have focused on small separations of distinctive varieties such as Pink Bourbon. This selection was picked at peak ripeness and milled on the farm before export.
